svc3.desc: Representing the interest of the Muslim community in legal and governmental affairs.

svc4.title: Educational Programs

svc5.title: Social Welfare

svc6.title: Conflict Resolution

svc7.title: Marriage Certificates

svc8.title: Hajj & Umrah Facilitation
svc8.desc: Official support letters and logistical guidance for sacred pilgrimages.

svc9.desc: Visa advisory services and official letters of support for religious and educational travel abroad.

svc10.title: Study Abroad Letters

history.heading: Our Origin Story
history.para1: The Supreme Council of Kenya Muslims (SUPKEM) was established in 1973 during a landmark General Conference held at Qur'an House on Mfangano Street in Nairobi.
history.para2: It was born out of a collective need to unify and coordinate the efforts of Kenya's diverse Muslim organizations, mosques, societies and community groups under one umbrella body. At a time when the Muslim community faced marginalization and lacked a structured platform for representation, SUPKEM emerged and continues to act as a voice of leadership, advocacy, and social development.

approaches.desc: We engage stakeholders through dialogue, partnerships, advocacy, empowerment, and collaboration to advance justice, peace, and development.

item1.title: Faith-Centered Dialogue
item1.desc: Open, inclusive approaches to promote mutual understanding and resolve conflicts within and beyond the community.

item2.title: Strategic Partnerships
item2.desc: Building alliances to mobilize resources, unify efforts, and co-create solutions for humanity.

item3.title: Community Empowerment
item3.desc: Influencing policy actions through education and capacity building to drive positive change.

approaches.commitBody: Through its enduring commitment to unity, justice and service, SUPKEM continues to provide moral and spiritual leadership while empowering communities across Kenya to confront challenges and pursue meaningful change for posterity.

leadership.subtitle: Our Purpose
leadership.heading: Strategic Leadership
leadership.desc: Mandated by its constitution, SUPKEM is structured to represent the interests of Muslims nationally, promote Islamic values, and support the aspirations of its member organizations.

item1.title: National Representation
item1.desc: Acting as the primary structured platform for Muslim representation to the Government and international partners.

item2.title: Social Development
item2.desc: Actively involved in humanitarian response, health initiatives, and civic education across all 47 counties.

item3.title: Justice & Advocacy
item3.desc: Advocating for the rights of the Muslim community while promoting interfaith harmony and peaceful coexistence.

meetLeaders.subtitle: Governance
meetLeaders.heading: Meet Our Leaders
meetLeaders.desc: Leading with integrity and dedication, the SUPKEM leadership is committed to representing the interests of the Muslim community in Kenya.

gallery.subtitle: A Legacy of Service
gallery.desc: Documenting our commitment to the community through leadership, dialogue, and grassroots initiatives across decades.

programs.subtitle: Operational Pillars
programs.title: Strategic Focus
programs.quote: Guided by four core pillars to deliver programmatic and institutional growth for the Ummah.

prog1.title: Programmes
prog1.desc: Implementing interventions in education, economic empowerment, health, peace building, and climate change.

prog2.title: Institutional Strengthening
prog2.desc: Building solid internal structures through robust governance, resource mobilization, and HR development.

prog3.title: Partnership & Collaboration
prog3.desc: Fostering strategic engagement with government, non-state actors, and faith-based institutions.

prog4.title: Cross Cutting Areas
prog4.desc: Ensuring effective delivery through comprehensive research, monitoring, evaluation, and strategic communication.

services.title: Digital Services
services.subtitle: Comprehensive digital frameworks designed to empower our community with efficiency and speed.

svc1.title: Marriage Registration
svc1.desc: Coordination of marriage officers under the Islamic faith.

svc5.desc: Coordinating Hajj and Umrah missions for Kenyan Muslims.

svc6.title: Membership Accreditation
svc6.desc: Recognition for organizations under our umbrella body.

Navbar.comingSoonDesc: We are currently engineering a full bilingual experience to better serve the global Muslim community. This feature will be launched as part of our upcoming Digital Transformation phase.

Footer.description: The Supreme Council of Kenya Muslims (SUPKEM) is the umbrella body of all Muslim organizations in Kenya. We are committed to fostering unity and progress.
Footer.address: Islamia House, Njugu Lane off Tom Mboya St., Nairobi, Kenya

About.description: The umbrella body of all Muslim organizations, societies, Mosque committees and groups in Kenya.
